description: Baseline harness setup: dockerized XHTTP XMUX benchmark scaffolding failed to execute (exit 127); diagnosing autoresearch script invocation.
Baseline benchmark ran in docker, but the autoresearch checks integration reported a false non-zero result; removing auto-check wrapper and validating tests manually instead.
Baseline XHTTP XMUX benchmark harness: added dockerized splithttp XMUX microbenchmarks and autoresearch docs/scripts.

Optimize XHTTP XMUX hot path with per-manager random selection and in-place cleanup; add safer xpadding/uplink defaults, preserve query params in Referer padding, and cover the fixes with new splithttp tests.

Further optimize XHTTP XMUX by merging unusable-client sweeping, eligible-client selection, and warm-refill accounting into one locked pass; preserve the config-default and xpadding fixes while keeping splithttp tests green.
Trim another XHTTP XMUX hot-path branch by dropping a redundant pre-selection warm-refill scheduling check; benchmark improves again while splithttp tests stay green.
Replace XHTTP XMUX reservoir-style selection with a one-pass round-robin cursor over the compacted usable client slice; this removes per-eligible RNG work while keeping splithttp tests green.
Trim XHTTP XMUX lock-path overhead by replacing the defer-based unlock in GetXmuxClient with explicit unlocks on each return path; splithttp tests remain green.
Tried inlining/reordering XMUX usability checks inside the sweep loop to avoid helper overhead, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting this hot-path reshuffle.

Add a lazy XHTTP XMUX fast path: perform full pool sweeps on a bounded interval, use cursor-based selection for normal acquisitions, and lazily remove bad entries encountered on the hot path; splithttp tests stay green.
Tried precomputing the XMUX warm target to shave scheduleWarmRefill overhead, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting this micro-optimization.
Tried splitting the lazy XMUX picker into separate concurrency/no-concurrency paths to remove a branch from the hot path, but it regressed slightly; reverting.
Tried relaxing the lazy XMUX health-sweep interval from 250ms to 500ms, but the benchmark regressed slightly; keeping the more conservative interval.
Tried reordering XMUX usability checks to avoid IsClosed on obviously spent clients, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting.
Tried initializing XMUX nextSweepAt during manager construction to skip the first sweep-zero check, but it regressed slightly; reverting.
Tried caching clientCount/connectionLimit locals in GetXmuxClient to trim repeated len/field reads, but the benchmark was slightly worse; reverting.

Improve lazy XHTTP XMUX selection by checking OpenUsage before the heavier usability probe on the fast path; saturated clients are skipped immediately while bounded sweeps still clean stale entries, and splithttp tests stay green.
Tried simplifying the lazy XMUX sweep-deadline branch by relying on `now.Before(nextSweepAt)` alone, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting.
Tried removing the redundant non-empty slice check from the lazy XMUX fast-path loop condition, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting.
Tried caching the lazy fast-path client count locally inside pickAvailableClientLocked to avoid repeated len() calls, but the benchmark regressed slightly; reverting.

Track approximate XHTTP XMUX usable-count state incrementally on client creation/removal/exhaustion and reconcile it on sweeps, so the hot path avoids threading usableCount through picker returns and warm-refill checks; splithttp tests stay green.
Only call XHTTP XMUX warm-refill scheduling after tracked client-count changes instead of every GetXmuxClient call; this slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Reorder XHTTP XMUX GetXmuxClient around an early hot-path return when a selected client exists and the pool is already at its connection target; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Add a direct first-candidate fast path in the lazy XHTTP XMUX picker before falling back to the full scan; the extra miss-path duplication slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Specialize XHTTP XMUX around config-level reuse/request/deadline flags and cached normalized ranges so the usability probe can skip disabled limits; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Replace per-call XHTTP XMUX sweep deadline polling with a timer-armed sweepDue flag, so the hot path only touches time.Now when a sweep is actually due or reusable-deadline configs need it; add coverage for off-cursor closed-client cleanup across the scheduled sweep boundary.

Cap XHTTP XMUX warmConnections against the sampled max-connections limit once in the manager constructor and reuse that stored target directly in warm fill/refill checks, trimming more hot-path bookkeeping after the timer-armed sweep change.

Split XHTTP XMUX warm-refill into an inlineable needsWarmRefillLocked check plus a separate scheduler to avoid entering the goroutine-launch helper on the common no-refill path; this was slightly worse than the new baseline, so discard.

Specialize the XHTTP XMUX usability probe to skip LeftRequests and reusable-deadline checks unless those limits are actually configured, using the existing config pointers instead of broader cached flag plumbing; splithttp tests stay green.

Inline and specialize the lazy XHTTP XMUX hot-loop usability checks directly inside pickAvailableClientLocked to avoid the helper call and repeated config probes; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Cache only the configured XHTTP XMUX request-limit and reusable-deadline booleans on the manager so pickXmuxClientLocked and isUsableClientLocked can avoid repeated config-pointer checks; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Add a no-cleanup fast path to lazy XHTTP XMUX cursor scanning that uses simple linear loops and only falls back to the mutating cleanup loop when a stale client is encountered; this was slightly worse than the current best, so discard.

Inline the lazy XHTTP XMUX pickXmuxClientLocked logic directly into GetXmuxClient to remove that helper call and keep sweep/time handling in one place; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Track whether the XHTTP XMUX warm target is already full and only enter warm-refill scheduling when the pool is below target or no refill is in flight; this slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Split the lazy XHTTP XMUX picker into a simple no-deadline/no-request-limit fast path and a full path, so the common per-client scan avoids extra request/deadline checks while configured limits still use the stricter path; splithttp tests stay green.

Further specialize the lazy XHTTP XMUX fast path for managers without a concurrency cap, so the common warm-pool selection loop skips the OpenUsage atomic entirely while concurrency-limited or request/deadline-limited cases still use the stricter picker variants; splithttp tests stay green.

Move XHTTP XMUX lazy fast-path dispatch into pickXmuxClientLocked so it picks the specialized picker variant up front and avoids an extra wrapper call plus redundant now.IsZero/request-limit branching on every acquisition; splithttp tests stay green.

Add an even narrower XHTTP XMUX no-concurrency fast path that directly returns the cursor client when it is usable and only falls back to the cleanup loop when that cursor entry is stale; this slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Inline the XHTTP XMUX warm-refill guard directly at GetXmuxClient call sites so the common full-pool path avoids calling scheduleWarmRefillLocked at all; this slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Further specialize the XHTTP XMUX lazy picker for managers without CMaxReuseTimes by adding closed-only variants for the no-concurrency and no-deadline/no-request-limit paths, so common default pools skip leftUsage checks while stricter variants still handle configured reuse/request/deadline limits; splithttp tests stay green.

Precompute a compact XHTTP XMUX picker mode in the manager constructor and switch on that mode in pickXmuxClientLocked instead of rechecking config pointers each call; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

In the XHTTP XMUX concurrency-specialized picker variants, cache the positive concurrency limit locally and remove the redundant per-iteration `m.concurrency > 0` guard inside loops that only run when a concurrency cap is already known to exist; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Only schedule XHTTP XMUX warm refills when usable-count state actually changes (lazy removals, sweep removals, or reuse exhaustion) instead of calling scheduleWarmRefillLocked on every successful selection; splithttp tests stay green.

Normalize zero/disabled XHTTP XMUX reuse/request/deadline ranges to nil once in the manager config copy so pickXmuxClientLocked and isUsableClientLocked can use simpler nil-only dispatch checks; this regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Refactor lazy closed-client removal logging and slice cleanup into a shared slow-path helper to shrink the hottest specialized XHTTP XMUX picker bodies; this was slightly worse than the current best Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Replace the specialized XHTTP XMUX picker loops’ scanned-vs-clientCount bookkeeping with a remaining-counter form in the hot no-reuse and closed-only paths; this substantially regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Attempted to add a fast XMUX closed-state path by caching per-client atomic closed flags, but the first pass left mux.go and benchmark plumbing in an inconsistent build state; fixing the refactor and retrying.

Cache an optional per-client atomic closed-state pointer in XmuxClient so the hot picker paths can call a tiny isClosed helper instead of repeatedly dispatching through XmuxConn.IsClosed; benchmark mocks and internal closable conns were updated to exercise the same path, but the full Dockerized xmux benchmark was still slightly worse than the current best, so discard.

Cache the xmuxClients slice header locally inside the two hottest specialized XHTTP XMUX lazy pickers and refresh it only after lazy removals, so the common no-removal path avoids repeated manager field reads/indexing while preserving cleanup behavior; splithttp tests stay green.

Tighten the two hottest specialized XHTTP XMUX picker loops a bit further by using equality-only wrap checks and a local nextClientIndex assignment when advancing the round-robin cursor; this was slightly worse than the new best Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Add an immutable XHTTP XMUX client-snapshot fast path for the common no-request-limit/no-deadline/no-reuse cases, using an atomic round-robin cursor plus an atomic sweepDue flag so GetXmuxClient can often return without taking the manager mutex; preserve existing lazy cleanup and warm-refill behavior by falling back to the locked picker as soon as a scanned candidate is closed or the pool still needs growth. splithttp tests stay green.

Replace the immutable XHTTP XMUX fast-path snapshot transport from atomic.Value to a typed atomic pointer holding an immutable snapshot struct, so GetXmuxClient keeps the same lock-free snapshot behavior and fallback-on-closed semantics while avoiding interface load/type-assert overhead on every hot-path acquisition; splithttp tests stay green.

Tried folding the atomic sweepDue gate into the immutable XMUX snapshot itself by niling the fast snapshot while a sweep is pending, so tryGetXmuxClientFast could skip a separate atomic Bool load; this preserved correctness but slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Split the immutable XMUX fast path into separate no-concurrency and concurrency helpers so the dispatcher can skip one hot branch and pass a cached concurrency local into the stricter scan; this substantially regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Shrink the immutable XHTTP XMUX fast-path round-robin cursor from atomic.Uint64 to atomic.Uint32; the cursor only needs wraparound semantics for pool selection, so this trims the hot Add/modulo path without changing snapshot selection behavior or the locked fallback logic. splithttp tests stay green.

Cache the fast-path connections and concurrency fields in locals inside tryGetXmuxClientFast so the lock-free XMUX snapshot scan avoids a few repeated manager field reads; this slightly regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Specialize the no-concurrency branch of the immutable XMUX fast path down to a direct cursor lookup plus closed check, removing its now-redundant one-iteration scan loop; this was slightly worse than the current best Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Extend the immutable XHTTP XMUX fast snapshot with cached power-of-two metadata (clientCount, indexMask, powerOfTwo) and use a mask-based index path when the current snapshot size is a power of two, while preserving the existing modulo fallback for other pool sizes; splithttp tests stay green.

Publish a nil immutable XMUX fast snapshot when the pool is actually empty, so the hot path can use the atomic pointer nil check as the empty-pool gate and avoid carrying a redundant zero-client snapshot case; preserve the existing power-of-two and fallback selection behavior, with splithttp tests still green.

Only publish the immutable XMUX fast snapshot when the fast path is actually eligible: the pool is non-empty, already at its sampled connection target when bounded, and no request-limit / reusable-deadline / reuse-count configs force the slow path. This moves those gates out of tryGetXmuxClientFast and trims the hot path to the sweep flag plus snapshot load before selection; splithttp tests stay green.

Tried pre-seeding the immutable XMUX fast-path round-robin cursor to MaxUint32 so tryGetXmuxClientFast could use atomic.Add(1) directly without the current subtract-one adjustment, but the Dockerized xmux benchmark was a touch worse than the new best, so discard.

Cache an optional directly-readable closed flag on each XMUX client when the underlying connection exposes one, and use that in the immutable fast path before falling back to XmuxConn.IsClosed. Real DefaultDialerClient now exposes its existing atomic closed flag, internal closable XMUX test conns use the same path, and the benchmark fake keeps the same always-open behavior while exposing the common production fast path; splithttp tests stay green.

Track on each immutable XMUX fast snapshot whether every published client has a direct closed flag, and use dedicated fast branches that call closedFlag.Load() directly without a per-client nil/fallback check when that common case holds; keep the interface fallback for mixed snapshots, with splithttp tests still green.

Tried flattening tryGetXmuxClientFast so the power-of-two + all-closed-flags snapshot case becomes a single top-level hot branch before the more general paths, but the reshaped control flow was slightly worse than the current best Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Tried replacing the snapshot powerOfTwo/allClosedFlags bool metadata with a compact bitfield and hot-case flag comparisons in tryGetXmuxClientFast, but that extra snapshot-flags plumbing regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Tried loading the immutable XMUX fast snapshot before checking sweepDueFlag and combining the nil/sweep gate into one early return branch, but that reshaped entry path was slightly worse than the current best Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Tried flipping the fast-path concurrency scans from `>= continue` to a `< return` shape so the successful path falls directly into the closed check and return, but that branch reshuffle regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

In the immutable XMUX fast path’s power-of-two + all-closed-flags + concurrency-limited branch, replace the per-iteration (nextIndex+scanned)&mask recomputation with an incrementing masked index cursor; preserve selection order and fallback-on-closed semantics, with splithttp tests still green.

Revisited fast-path local concurrency caching in the new snapshot context by caching m.concurrency only inside the hottest power-of-two + all-closed-flags branch, but that narrower variant still regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Tried inverting the all-closed-flags fast-path closed check to return on `!closedFlag.Load()` instead of branching on the rare closed case first, but that reshaped branch direction regressed the Dockerized xmux benchmark, so discard.

Publish and consult a second immutable XMUX fast-snapshot pointer dedicated to the common power-of-two + all-closed-flags case, so tryGetXmuxClientFast can skip loading the general snapshot and skip the allClosedFlags/powerOfTwo dispatch checks on that hottest path while preserving the existing general immutable snapshot fallback for other eligible cases; splithttp tests stay green.
